title: EXTEKK Luxe — Discord security bot landing page (4-agent)

spec.label: architect
spec.prompt:
You are designing a re-imagined, breathtaking landing page for EXTEKK — a premium Discord security bot. The original site (extekk.mysellauth.com) sells the bot but looks generic-hacker. Your version must look BRUTALLY LUXURIOUS — Linear × Apple × Discord × terminal aesthetics fused into a single, confident, hacker-couture site.

Key product facts (from the original site):
- EXTEKK is a Discord security & server-insurance bot.
- Restores nuked servers in 1 second (channels, categories, roles, vouches).
- AI engine for scam-prevention + auto-moderation.
- Features: OAuth2 Member & Server Restore, 1-click Aesthetic Server Builder & Lockdown, Pro Ticket System, EXTEKK Sentinel (auto-mod patrolling chat 24/7, deletes phishing, mutes spammers), Universal Vouch Vault & Reputation, Global Scammer Blacklist.
- Stats: 23.4K bot downloads, 11.6K premium customers, 24/7 active support, 5-star overall.
- Pricing tiers: Free + Premium Free Trial.
- Discord-server-driven distribution (Discord CTA front and centre).

The new site must NOT be a shop — it's a marketing landing with one job: get visitors to add the bot to their Discord. No checkout, no Stripe.

Output a tight Markdown spec with EXACTLY these section headings, in this order:

## Brand promise
2 sentences. The promise EXTEKK makes in plain language.

## Tone
3 adjectives describing the writing voice. Hacker-couture, security-first, no fluff.

## Information architecture
List every section in order — section name, one-line purpose. Aim for 8-10 sections (hero, watch-it-work, numbers, feature grid, terminal demo, testimonials, pricing, FAQ, final CTA, footer).

## Hero copy
Headline (max 8 words, breath-taking), sub-headline (one sentence ≤ 18 words), primary CTA label (≤ 3 words), secondary CTA label (≤ 3 words), uptime/status pill text.

## Watch-it-work — 3 steps
For each of the three steps, give a one-line state name and one-line description: ATTACK DETECTED → RESTORE INITIATED → SERVER REBUILT. Make it feel like watching a heist movie.

## Live numbers
4 counter cards. For each: label + final number + supporting unit (e.g. 'servers protected · 23,400+'). Numbers animate from 0 to the final on viewport entry.

## Feature grid — 6 cards
For each: 1-2 word title, one-line description (≤ 14 words), one keyword for the icon (e.g. 'shield', 'terminal', 'eye', 'ticket', 'badge', 'globe'). Every card titled in ALL CAPS.

## Terminal demo — copy for code blocks
Three side-by-side terminal blocks. Each block: a `$ ` shell prompt followed by 2-4 output lines that look like real EXTEKK commands. Use commands like `extekk restore`, `extekk lockdown`, `extekk vouches sync`. Output should feel like real CLI — timestamps, success ticks, durations.

## Testimonials — 3 fixed quotes
Not a marquee, three confident quotes fixed in a row. For each: a 2-sentence quote in voice (mostly Discord-seller / community-mod tone), the persona (`@handle`, role like 'Server owner · 12K members'), and a small green check 'verified purchase' tag.

## Pricing — 2 tiers
Free (everything that exists today) and Pro (unspecified premium — 'starts at $0' free trial, then real number — say $19/mo). For each tier: header line, 6 feature bullets (✓ or ✗), single CTA label.

## FAQ — 5 questions
Q&A pairs. Real concerns: 'Is it safe to give the bot OAuth?', 'How fast is restore?', 'What if Discord deletes my server?', 'Do you log my server data?', 'Can I cancel anytime?'. Answers ≤ 30 words each, confident not defensive.

## Final CTA
One line invitation + button label. ≤ 12 words total.

## Footer
Navigation links (5 items), social (Discord, GitHub), legal (Terms / Privacy / Refund). Plus a single hacker-tagline above copyright (≤ 8 words).

## Color palette
Three hex codes:
- Obsidian background (pure or near-pure black, ≤ #0a0a0a luminance)
- Electric accent (refined neon mint-green or cyan — NOT the toxic #00ff00 of the original)
- Discord blue (#5865F2 — used sparingly for the brand-aligned CTA)
Also pick a subtle red for 'alert/breach detected' state.

## Type stack
Two Google Fonts. Pick a body sans (Geist or Inter) + a mono (JetBrains Mono or Geist Mono). Justify in one sentence.

Be opinionated. No hedging. Every design choice should feel inevitable.

spec.label: designer
spec.prompt:
OUTPUT MODE: TEXT ONLY. No tools. No write_file. No sub-agent delegation. Respond with raw text in the format below, nothing else.

You are the lead designer for EXTEKK Luxe. The architect's spec defines IA + copy. Your job: turn it into a brutally luxurious visual system for the dark, hacker-security genre — Linear × Apple × Discord × terminal aesthetics fused. Output ONLY these sections:

## Logo
A self-contained <svg viewBox='0 0 320 80'>. The wordmark 'EXTEKK' in display sans + a tiny green-accent geometric mark on the left (a stylized shield, terminal cursor, or an angular insignia — pick the most unforgettable). White wordmark on transparent background. NO emoji, NO smiley faces. Output the full SVG ready to paste.

## Color tokens
A CSS :root block with: --bg-0 (pure obsidian), --bg-1 (raised surface), --bg-2 (panel), --fg-0 (pure white), --fg-1 (75% white), --fg-2 (45% white), --accent (architect's mint/cyan), --accent-glow (rgba 60% accent), --discord (#5865F2), --alert (architect's red), --border (rgba 8% white), --grid (rgba 4% accent for the background grid). Plus radius scale (--r-1 4px, --r-2 8px, --r-3 12px, --r-4 16px, --r-pill 999px) and spacing scale (--s-1 4px through --s-7 96px in a clean ladder).

## Typography
@import line for the two architect-picked Google Fonts and a base scale (h1, h2, h3, body, mono, micro) with concrete sizes + line-heights + letter-spacing. h1 must clamp() between 56 and 112 px. Body 17 px. Mono 14 px.

## Layout sketches — three states
For each, STATE NAME + 2-sentence purpose + a self-contained inline <svg viewBox='0 0 1280 720'>. Use real labels, real numbers, real button copy from the architect's hero copy. SVGs ≥ 1280×720, no placeholder text:
  1. Hero (full viewport, headline + sub + 2 CTAs + animated terminal panel on the right + grid background)
  2. Watch-it-work (3 horizontal steps with arrow connectors, ATTACK DETECTED → RESTORE INITIATED → SERVER REBUILT, with timing pills 0.000s / 0.420s / 1.000s)
  3. Pricing (two cards side-by-side, Free vs Pro, feature matrix with ✓/✗, distinct elevation)

## Motion
6 specific animations with cubic-bezier curves and durations:
  - hero terminal typing (0.06s per char, no easing)
  - hero grid pulse (4s ease-in-out infinite, very subtle)
  - number counter roll-up (1.2s cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1) on viewport entry)
  - feature card glow-on-hover (260 ms cubic-bezier(0.33, 1, 0.68, 1))
  - watch-it-work step transition (450 ms cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1) staggered)
  - alert pulse on the breach indicator (1.2s ease-in-out infinite)

## Mood
3 sentences. Confident, premium, hacker-couture — the visual equivalent of a tailored bulletproof vest. Not noisy, not toxic neon — refined.

## Spec
{{architect}}

spec.label: engineer
spec.prompt:
Implement the EXTEKK Luxe landing page as a single static site — no framework, no build step. Pure HTML + CSS + JS that drops into /var/www/extekk-luxe/ and serves with Caddy.

For every file, output a fenced code block whose FIRST line is a comment with the file path:
  - HTML: `<!-- index.html -->`
  - CSS: `/* style.css */`
  - JS: `// script.js`
  - SVG: `<!-- logo.svg -->`
  - JSON: `<!-- manifest.json -->` then the JSON

Files, in order: index.html, style.css, script.js, logo.svg, manifest.json, robots.txt.

Hard requirements (use VERBATIM from upstream where indicated):
  - Color tokens from {{designer}} verbatim — paste the :root block as the first thing in style.css.
  - Logo SVG from {{designer}} verbatim into logo.svg.
  - Typography pairing from {{designer}} (the @import + scale).
  - All on-screen copy (headline, sub, CTAs, status pill, watch-it-work step labels + timings, feature titles + descriptions, terminal commands, testimonials with handles, pricing tier features, FAQ Q&A, final CTA) from {{architect}} verbatim.

Sections to implement, in order, matching the architect's IA:
  1. Sticky glass-morphism nav (backdrop-filter: blur(16px), 1px bottom border at --border) — logo left, links centre, primary CTA right, mobile burger.
  2. Hero — clamp(56px,8vw,112px) headline with subtle gradient text fill (white → 70% white), sub-headline beneath, two buttons (primary accent fill + secondary outline), uptime pill, animated terminal panel on the right showing the architect's terminal demo lines with 0.06s-per-char typing animation. Subtle grid background using a CSS repeating-linear-gradient at --grid color.
  3. Watch-it-work — three horizontal cards with arrow connectors. Each card has a state name, a one-line description, and an animated timing pill (0.000s, 0.420s, 1.000s) that counts up when scrolled into view. Use IntersectionObserver.
  4. Live numbers — 4 counter cards animating from 0 to the architect's final values on viewport entry (1.2s cubic-bezier).
  5. Feature grid — 6 cards in a 3-column grid (mobile: 1 column). Each card has an inline-SVG icon (use a clean stroke-only set — Lucide-style for shield / terminal / eye / ticket / badge / globe), a title in ALL CAPS, a one-line description, and a hover glow using box-shadow with --accent-glow.
  6. Terminal demo — three side-by-side terminal panels (mobile: stacked) with macOS-style window chrome (three colored dots top-left). Each panel shows the architect's commands + outputs. Mono font, syntax-coloured prompts and success ticks.
  7. Testimonials — three fixed cards in a row. Quote, persona handle, role line, verified-purchase pill. NOT a marquee.
  8. Pricing — two cards side-by-side. Free vs Pro. Feature matrix with ✓/✗ icons. Pro card has a subtle accent glow and 'most popular' tag.
  9. FAQ — five accordion items using <details><summary>. Open one by default.
 10. Final CTA — single huge-text invitation + Discord-blue button.
 11. Footer — wordmark, hacker tagline, navigation, social, legal, copyright.

Global:
  - prefers-reduced-motion respected (disable typing, counters, pulses).
  - Mobile responsive at 768 / 480 breakpoints.
  - Smooth scroll on anchor links.
  - All interactive elements have visible focus rings (--accent outline).
  - SEO: <meta name='description'>, OG tags, theme-color = the accent.
  - Manifest: name 'EXTEKK', short_name 'EXTEKK', start_url '/', display 'standalone', theme_color = accent, background_color = --bg-0.

No TODO. No placeholder strings. No 'add real content here'. Real working page on first load.

## Spec
{{architect}}

## Design
{{designer}}

spec.label: operator
spec.prompt:
OUTPUT MODE: TEXT ONLY. No tools (no write_file, no read_file, no run_shell_command, no sub-agent delegation). Respond with raw text only.

Write the project's README.md and a single deploy snippet. Output as fenced blocks with file-path first-line comments:

  <!-- README.md -->
  Title 'EXTEKK Luxe'
  Tagline (≤ 12 words, hacker-couture)
  3-sentence what-it-is and how-it-was-built (mention ClaudeGravity 4-agent pipeline)
  '## Live' section pointing to https://claudegravity.site
  '## Run locally' section: open index.html in any browser
  Browser support note (modern Chromium / Firefox / Safari 16+)
  '## Source provenance' note that this is a re-imagined version of extekk.mysellauth.com — design + code generated end-to-end through ClaudeGravity
  License: MIT one-liner

  <!-- deploy.sh -->
  #!/bin/bash
  Single rsync line uploading all files to root@46.36.39.161:/var/www/extekk-luxe/, plus an `ssh hukot 'systemctl reload caddy'` line at the end.

Keep it tight. No prose between the blocks.
